hypothesis: Retraining generative models on chemically accurate valency representations (Kekulized datasets derived from corrected valency calculations) will produce GEOM-Drugs molecules whose GFN2-xTB–optimized geometries exhibit smaller bond length, angle, and torsion deviations (Δr, Δθ, Δφ) and near-zero relaxation energy (ΔE_relax) compared with molecules generated by models trained on original valency data, indicating higher chemical realism.
